code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Multi-purchase failures with safety hazards
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept
    - Negative updates invalidating previous satisfaction
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defects

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Persistent frustration despite occasional success
    - "Better alternatives exist" comparisons with justification
    - Regret expressed through cost/value complaints

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Brief factual positivity without enthusiasm
    - Lingering functional drawbacks post-resolution
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise enthusiastic praise ("Works perfectly!") 
    - 3+ functional aspects praised despite 1 non-functional flaw
    - Close comparisons to top brands with satisfaction
    - Long-term validation overcoming initial skepticism
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Nearly matches X" →5 if satisfaction expressed
    - Functional praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Functional praise + functional flaw →4 (unless 3+ strengths)
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Brief effectiveness statements →4 unless enthusiastic
    - Setup difficulties →5 if core function flawless post-setup
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Perfect fit! Solved our storage needs completely."
<Reasoning>Concise enthusiastic praise without negatives</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Nearly matches premium brands in daily use!" 
<Reasoning>Close comparison with satisfaction despite minor differences</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Vibrant colors and durable, but requires ironing"
<Reasoning>Non-functional drawback with multiple strengths</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Does the job adequately for light use"
<Reasoning>Brief factual statement lacking enthusiasm</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Works after 2 washes but edges stay stiff"
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback post-resolution</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Constantly retighten screws - not worth effort"
<Reasoning>Excessive maintenance for basic function</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Good for guests but too flimsy for daily use"
<Reasoning>Contextual limitation with compromise</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Initially confusing buttons, now cleans flawlessly"
<Reasoning>Minor learning curve with core excellence</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Sturdy construction let down by sizing issues"
<Reasoning>Functional flaw outweighs single strength</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
artifacts_json:

  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (26 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 11
  gold 5 -> 4: 5
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 5 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "This machine seems determined to foil every effort I make to clean my carpets. The bottom section never works. It's job is to collect the dirty water, but since the top section hardly puts any water out at all, it doesn't have much to do. I have labo..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This is an ok bin. It holds its shape fairly well and is nice and sturdy. There is no side support, so you can't stack something on the top. Color is a nice light pink-nothing too flashy."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 26 errors, 22 borderline out of 80 examples."}
